introduction:
A Death Certificate in Tamil Nadu is issued by the local Municipal Corporation, Municipality, Town Panchayat, or Village Panchayat under the Tamil Nadu Registration of Births and Deaths Rules. It is legal proof of death recording name, date, place, and cause of death.

Essential for insurance claims, bank account settlement, property mutation, pension processing, and succession proceedings. Tamil Nadu uses the TN Urban e-Pay portal for urban areas and CRS portal for digital registration.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Register within 21 days (free)
  • After 21 days: late fee + Registrar permission
  • After 1 year: District Registrar / Magistrate order
  • First copy is FREE; duplicates ₹5–₹50
  • Track at tnurbantree.tn.gov.in or crsorgi.gov.in
  • Helpline: 1800-102-5553 (Toll-Free)
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Note: Carry originals; submit self-attested copies.
Proof of Death
  • MCCD from hospital
  • Hospital death summary
  • Cremation / Burial receipt
  • FIR (unnatural death)
ID of Deceased
  • Aadhaar
  • Voter ID
  • PAN
  • Passport
  • Driving Licence
  • Ration Card
Informant Documents
  • Aadhaar of informant
  • Relationship proof
  • Address proof
For Delayed Registration
  • Affidavit
  • District Registrar / Magistrate order (1 year+)
  • Non-availability certificate
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via TN e-Sevai / CRS

Tamil Nadu provides online registration via TN e-Sevai, TN Urban e-Pay, and the national CRS portal.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Portal
    Go to tnurbanepay.tn.gov.in or crsorgi.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Create account, verify via OTP.
  3. Select Death Registration
    Navigate to Services → Birth/Death → Register Death.
  4. Fill Form
    Enter deceased details, cause and place of death, informant info, ULB name.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload MCCD, ID proof. Max 200 KB
  6. Submit
    Note Reference Number.
  7. Verification
    Local Registrar verifies within 7–15 days.
Tip: Greater Chennai Corporation has a separate portal at chennaicorporation.gov.in.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Register Offline at Municipal / Panchayat Office

Visit the Municipal Corporation, Municipality, Town Panchayat, or Village Panchayat office.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Office
    Hours: Mon–Sat, 10 AM – 5 PM
  2. Collect Form
    Get Form No. 2 — free.
  3. Fill & Attach
    Fill form, attach photocopies, carry originals.
  4. Submit & Get Receipt
    Receive Acknowledgement.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect within 7–15 working days.
Processing Time: 7–15 working days.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Status

Visit TN Urban e-Pay or CRS portal.

Download Certificate

Download digitally signed certificate from the portal.

Legal Use

Insurance, property, bank, pension, and succession matters.

Grievance

Call 1800-102-5553 or visit local municipal office.
